summary: Add the first authenticated rust-diffforge desktop dashboard shell with workspace navigation, placeholder terminal panes, right-side tabs, settings, and sign out.

review_items.summary: Authenticated desktop users see a simple dashboard instead of the temporary logged-in text.
The authenticated React state renders workspaces, terminal placeholders, a right-side tab panel, settings, and sign-out controls.
Desktop startup shows a branded logo loading screen while the app checks the backend and restores any saved session.
After a session becomes authenticated, the shell briefly shows an initializing workspace state before rendering the dashboard.
Startup auth checks must stop blocking the launch screen within 5 seconds and show login when no restorable desktop session is available.
Saved desktop session validation is bounded to a 5 second startup timeout before clearing the local session and returning to web login.
Authenticated desktop users must see the pricing-only screen unless the server-returned desktop session user has planStatus paid.
Desktop session exchange and validation responses refresh the user's billing status from Stripe subscription cache before returning planStatus.
Desktop session responses continue returning user.planStatus, with paid derived from active or trialing Stripe subscriptions and all other states treated as free.
No auth token, deep-link, API, or database contract changes are introduced by this dashboard shell.

rules: The dashboard must only render when authState is authenticated.
The dashboard must only render when the desktop session user has planStatus paid.
Free, missing, unknown, stale, or failed billing states must show the pricing-only desktop screen instead of the dashboard.
Desktop paid/free status must come from the server-returned desktop session user, not from client-provided state.
The pricing-only desktop screen may open the hosted pricing page in the browser and refresh the desktop session status.
The launch screen must not read or write auth data beyond the existing session restoration flow.
The initializing workspace state must be visual only until explicit workspace loading APIs or local commands are added.
The launch screen must not wait indefinitely on deep-link listener registration or session validation.
Saved desktop session validation must either restore a session or fall back to the signed-out web login state within 5 seconds.
If no saved session exists, or a saved session cannot be restored, the signed-out login screen must be shown.
Settings and sign out must remain available from the authenticated shell.
Terminal, workspace, file, web, and policy panels are placeholders only and must not imply working project data until backed by explicit APIs or local commands.
The signed-out web-login flow must remain available and unchanged for unauthenticated users.

cross_project_impact.contract: Desktop session exchange and validation return user.planStatus derived from server billing state. rust-diffforge gates dashboard access on planStatus paid.
cross_project_impact.compatibility_notes: Free desktop sessions remain valid but render pricing only. Paid desktop sessions continue into the dashboard after workspace initialization.

tests.summary: Validate Next.js desktop session and billing helper changes.
Validate the desktop React shell builds.
